routes.title: BhuMe, Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe 1 Understand 2 Playground 3 The task 4 Get started 5 Test 6 Submit TAKE-HOME ASSIGNMENT 20.26°N 74.03°E · Maharashtra The boundary on the map isn't where the land is. Maharashtra's land-record maps were drawn on paper, then georeferenced onto satellite imagery, so the official plot outlines sit metres off the real fields. Your job: find where each one really sits, and know when you can't. Thanks for applying — this page is the take-home. There's no separate doc to download: read it, try the real data in the playground, and submit when you're ready. Start the take-home Open the playground ~8–12 HRS Completeness isn't the bar A take-home, not a sprint. Work at your own pace; you choose where to stop. USE AI FREELY We assess how you direct it Lean on it where it helps. Steering a model well is the skill we're reading. JUDGED ON How you think, not how much you cover Depth of reasoning over breadth of coverage. Honest limits count for you. BhuMe · land records An engineering take-home

routes.title: Understand · BhuMe Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe 1 Understand 2 Playground 3 The task 4 Get started 5 Test 6 Submit ON THIS SHEET 01 What a plot is 02 How the map was made 03 Two kinds of wrong 04 What you're given 05 Glossary UNDERSTAND: THE GROUND-TRUTH PROBLEM Land that's been on the map for a century, just not in the right place. No geospatial background needed. This page takes you from zero to why the official plot boundaries are wrong, and what "wrong" even means here. THE ONE-SENTENCE TASK For each land plot, decide whether the official boundary can be nudged onto the real field, and if so, where it should go. 01 / What a plot is A plot is one drawn outline on the cadastral map, and the unit you correct. It's what BhuNaksha, the state's public land-map website, labels a Plot No; in your data each one carries a unique plot_number. Alongside the map, each plot has a written record, the 7/12 extract (saat-baara, literally "seven-twelve"). It records who holds the land, the holders (or khatedars), and how much area is on fil

routes.title: Playground · BhuMe Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe ✓ Understand 2 Playground 3 The task 4 Get started 5 Test 6 Submit Leaflet | Imagery © Google Vadnerbhairav Malatavadi PROVIDED LAYERS Bundle imagery imagery.tif Field hints ROUGH DOWNLOAD BUNDLE input.geojson imagery.tif boundaries.tif Vadnerbhairav Nashik Click any plot to inspect it. Then Move it onto the field in the imagery, or Reshape its outline, the area recomputes live, so you can feel where the record and the ground disagree. Click a plot, then Move or Reshape it, area recomputes live. No auto-align; that's yours to build. Next: the task →

routes.title: The task · BhuMe Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe ✓ Understand ✓ Playground 3 The task 4 Get started 5 Test 6 Submit THE SPEC 01 In, out & method 02 How far you take it 03 How it's scored 04 What you send back THE TASK: CONTRACT & RUBRIC Correct the plots. Know when you can't. You choose the approach entirely, we only fix the shape of what you read and return so it can be scored fairly. But we're judging a method, not a pile of hand-fixed plots, so you submit the code that produces your output, and we run and read it. 01 / In, out & method You get the whole village at once, not one plot at a time, so lean on as much or as little context as you like. Your code reads the bundle, the plots in input.geojson plus the imagery.tif (and the optional boundary hints), and writes predictions.geojson beside it. Both .geojson files are FeatureCollections in EPSG:4326 (the standard lon/lat coordinate system). For every plot you make a claim about, return: FIELD MEANING plot_number Echo the plot's id, exactly. status CORRECTED you moved it · F

routes.title: Get started · BhuMe Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe ✓ Understand ✓ Playground ✓ The task 4 Get started 5 Test 6 Submit GET STARTED: DATA & KIT Two villages. One kit. Everything you need to begin. Pick a village to work, or both. Each bundle is small enough to open and poke at by hand. The starter kit handles the geospatial plumbing so you can spend your time on the actual problem. Vadnerbhairav NASHIK 2,457 PLOTS 54.2km² EXTENT 7,753m² MEDIAN PLOT Open agrarian land, large, well-separated fields. The drift is easy to see, so it’s the place to build intuition first. BUNDLE input.geojson 2.3 MB imagery.tif 13.6 MB boundaries.tif ROUGH example_truths.geojson 6 plots Malatavadi KOLHAPUR 2,508 PLOTS 5.8km² EXTENT 872m² MEDIAN PLOT Mixed, tightly-packed parcels near a town. Smaller fields and crowded edges, where your confidence has to earn its keep. BUNDLE input.geojson 1.6 MB imagery.tif 6.9 MB boundaries.tif ROUGH example_truths.geojson 3 plots Python starter kit Thin, readable helpers, not a framework. Read it, then ignore it if you

routes.title: Test · BhuMe Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe ✓ Understand ✓ Playground ✓ The task ✓ Get started 5 Test 6 Submit + − Leaflet | Imagery © Google Next: submit → TEST: SELF-SCORE Score it before you send it. Drop your predictions.geojson in. We overlay it on the example truths and score it with the same metrics we grade on, locally, in your browser. Vadnerbhairav Malatavadi Drop or choose predictions.geojson scored against 6 example truths in Vadnerbhairav Upload your predictions to see the scorecard. hidden truth official (start) your prediction

routes.title: Submit · BhuMe Boundary Take-Home
routes.textSample: BhuMe ✓ Understand ✓ Playground ✓ The task ✓ Get started ✓ Test 6 Submit SUBMIT: HAND-IN Send us how you think. Your whole submission is one GitHub repo (code, predictions, transcripts) plus a short Google Form. We're hiring for how you think, so it's your approach we judge, not your score: the metrics rank submissions but don't make the call. Spend your effort on the reasoning, not on squeezing the last point. In the repo, public or private with us invited: 1 Your code The method that turns a village bundle (the plots in input.geojson plus the imagery) into your predictions, runnable by us. Rough is fine; we run it and read it. No written report, just the video and your transcripts. 2 Predictions A predictions.geojson for each village you attempted, in the contract format. Run it through Test first to catch schema issues. 3 Your AI transcripts ★ HOW YOU DIRECT AI We expect you to lean on AI two ways: to get your head around an unfamiliar problem (web chats), and to build the solution

uploadTests.scoreText: scored against 6 example truths in Vadnerbhairav 4 corrected · 2 flagged · of 6 truths MEDIAN IOU (YOU) 0.888 official 0.612 IMPROVEMENT +0.233 100% of plots improved ACCURATE @ IOU≥.5 100% median centroid err 5.4 m CALIBRATION Ρ 0.40 ★ rank corr, −1 to 1 · AUC needs a hit and a miss too RESTRAINT N/A graded on the hidden set, no controls here Median IoU your typical overlap with the truth, 0–1 (shared ÷ combined area), vs the official start · improvement IoU gained over official · accurate share of your fixes clearing IoU 0.5 (a solid overlap) · centroid err distance between the two centres · calibration does your confidence rank good fixes above bad (0.5 = chance, 1 = perfect) · restraint did you avoid moving already-correct plots. These are the public example truths only, a handful, so treat the numbers as a rough directional check, not a grade . Calibration especially needs more plots to mean much, so reason about what your confidence should represent rather than maximizing it here. Your real grade uses a larger hidden set, don't overfit to these few. hidden truth official (start) your prediction
